title: General Aptitude - Verbal Aptitude - Incorrect Sentence Part

questions.question:
\( \underset{\text{I}}{\underline{\text{While trying to collect}}} \) an envelope \( \underset{\text{II}}{\underline{\text{from under the table,}}} \) \( \underset{\text{III}}{\underline{\text{Mr. X fell down}}} \) and \( \underset{\text{IV}}{\underline{\text{was losing consciousness.}}} \)

Which one of the above underlined parts of the sentence is NOT appropriate?



GATE CSE 2014 Set 3 | Question: GA-1

Which one of the parts (A, B, C, D) in the sentence contains an ERROR?

No sooner had the doctor seen the results of the blood test, than he suggested the patient to see the specialist.



GATE2012 AR: GA-4

One of the parts \( (A, B, C, D) \) in the sentence given below contains an ERROR. Which one of the following is INCORRECT?

I requested that he should be given the driving test today instead of tomorrow.



GATE2012 CY: GA-2

All engineering students should learn mechanics, mathematics and how to do computation.

                I                            II                                            III                           IV

Which of the above underlined parts of the sentence is not appropriate?



GATE2013 AE: GA-4

\( \underset{\text{I}}{\underline{\text{The professor}}} \) \( \underset{\text{II}}{\underline{\text{ordered to}}} \) \( \underset{\text{III}}{\underline{\text{the students to go}}} \) \( \underset{\text{IV}}{\underline{\text{out of the class.}}} \)

Which of the above underlined parts of the sentence is grammatically incorrect?



GATE2013 CE: GA-2
